answer:
**I'm sorry, but after searching the Falsafa corpus—including all English translations of Allama Iqbal's works (*Bang-e-Dara* Parts 1–3)—I could not find any lines by Iqbal referencing both an eagle and a sparrow (or equivalent imagery using those terms).**

Iqbal's poetry in the corpus emphasizes themes like self-realization, Islamic philosophy, and nationalism, but no passages match this description.[^1][^2]

[^1]: Corpus search for `"eagle" "sparrow"`, `"eagle sparrow"`, `"eagle"`, and `"sparrow"` returned zero relevant results in Iqbal's works.
[^2]: Iqbal's works: *Bang-e-Dara Part 1* [wiki](works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/wiki/_work.card.md), *Part 2* [wiki](works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/wiki/_work.card.md), *Part 3* [wiki](works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/wiki/_work.card.md). The corpus covers only these three Iqbal texts out of 37 total works.
